source: AskWoltAI MCP / ask-wolt-mcp schema.py (2026), incl. reporting churn tables — replace with richer examples later

snowflake_hints.classic_vs_wolt_plus: F_PURCHASES: IS_SUBSCRIPTION FALSE/TRUE; F_UNIT_ECONOMICS: SUBSCRIPTION_FEE_LOCAL > 0 implies Wolt+; reconciliation: IS_SUBSCRIPTION on F_UNIT_ECONOMICS_RECONCILIATION
snowflake_hints.wolt_for_work: F_PURCHASES: SEGMENT = 'B2B' with BUSINESS_LINE = 'Wolt for Work'; may need BUSINESS_LINE_CONFIGURATION join in reconciliation
snowflake_hints.drive: F_PURCHASES: IS_DRIVE = TRUE; reconciliation: BUSINESS_LINE = 'Drive' or DRIVE_REVENUE_RECON > 0
snowflake_hints.preorder: F_PURCHASES: IS_PREORDER = TRUE (orthogonal to surface — often scored with primary product)
snowflake_hints.reporting_churn_analytics: Pre-aggregated churn and lifecycle metrics: PRODUCTION.MERCHANT.VENUE_COUNTRY_REPORTING_MONTHLY (portfolio / Looker-style monthly churn KPIs), _WEEKLY (weekly grain), _DAILY (day-level movement and venue-level churn lists with CHURN_DATE, IS_SOFT_CHURNED / IS_DELETED / IS_UNPUBLISHED, STATUS_DELETED_REASON, STATUS_UNPUBLISHED_REASON). Same column names across grains; filters typically PERIOD = month|week|day and METRIC_LEVEL = venue_id. For churn-only counts/rates, do not join D_FX_RATES. Dim-level venue flags (e.g. IS_SOFT_CHURNED, IS_VENUE_CHURNED) on D_VENUES are documented separately in schema.py — use reporting tables for labelled churn flows and denominators. Full SQL patterns: ask-wolt-mcp/schema.py sections CHURN / VENUE LIFECYCLE and VENUE-LEVEL CHURN WITH REASONS.

text:
Enterprise (ENT) restaurant churn — interim definition (per Wolt product surface)

Scope: Score churn separately for each major customer-facing product line at the venue/merchant level where ENT applies. Surfaces to model independently:

• Classic — Non–Wolt+ orders (standard marketplace delivery/takeaway; IS_SUBSCRIPTION = FALSE / no Wolt+ fee attribution).
• Wolt+ — Subscription member orders (IS_SUBSCRIPTION TRUE or SUBSCRIPTION_FEE_LOCAL > 0 on economics tables as documented in schema).
• Takeaway / pickup — Customer collection (DELIVERY_METHOD / takeaway paths; distinct commission tier vs homedelivery in reporting).
• Wolt Drive — B2B delivery-as-a-service (IS_DRIVE; Drive business line / drive revenue).
• Wolt for Work — B2B employee-benefit / corporate meals (SEGMENT B2B + Wolt for Work business line).
• Pre-order — Advance orders (IS_PREORDER); usually layered on top of another surface for churn, not a standalone P&L line everywhere.

Soft churn (per surface): Venue remains published and active, but delivered orders on that surface drop to zero (e.g. last 28 days) while other surfaces may still have volume — reversible operational decay.

Hard churn (per surface): Surface permanently disabled or venue unpublished/closed for that capability after prior activity — e.g. deleted/unpublished venue, takeaway turned off after historical takeaway orders, or terminal loss of Drive/WfW program eligibility.

Operational / non-churn: Temporary closure, renovation, or data issues — use hours, support, and config signals in the explainer.

Warehouse alignment: For market-level churn KPIs and net churn venues, the MERCHANT venue-country reporting tables (monthly/weekly/daily grain) document the same churn columns; for individual churned venues with delete/unpublish reasons and CHURN_DATE, use the daily reporting table as described in AskWoltAI schema.py — not the raw churn_mart.* placeholders in this repo’s sql/ templates.

Replace this blob when you have labelled churn examples; keep product_surfaces aligned with ProductCode in code.
